claim: ESM2Extractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor to compute mean-pooled 1280-dimensional per-protein embeddings from the pretrained facebook/esm2_t33_650M_UR50D weights. Heavy deps (torch, transformers) are imported inside _ensure_loaded — module-top imports are clean. Output contract is a DataFrame indexed by locus_tag with 1280 esm2_650M_dim_{i} float columns. The cache parquet is not populated by this session; the Colab notebook notebooks/populate_tier1_cache.ipynb runs the extractor on GPU and writes cell_sim/features/cache/esm2_650M.parquet.
source_detail: Session 14. The extractor is verifiable by reading the module + running the 6 sandbox-safe tests in cell_sim/tests/test_esm2_extractor.py. Pretrained weights cited in the module docstring: Lin et al. 2023 Science 379:1123, 'Evolutionary-scale prediction of atomic-level protein structure' (ESM-2 family); facebook/esm2_t33_650M_UR50D on HuggingFace Hub (~650M parameters, 33 layers, 1280 embedding dim). Those external weights are NOT registered as a memory_bank source — the fact only documents the wrapper API.

context.commit_range: TBD — will be filled in once Session 14 push lands.

caveats: The cache parquet is empty until the Colab notebook is run. Callers that join with this source before population get an NaN block (FeatureRegistry.join_features contract).
The module is pure plumbing; no MCC measurement is claimed here. A follow-up fact (facts/measured/mcc_with_tier1_features.json) will document any classifier built on top of the cached embeddings.
ESM-2 context window is 1024 tokens. The longest known Syn3A CDS is ~900 aa, so truncation should not fire in practice; it is handled with a warning path in the extractor if it does.

The extractor's write_cache() uses the default path cell_sim/features/cache/ unless the caller passes cache_dir. Users who put parquets elsewhere must register them via FeatureRegistry with the correct parquet_path.
